binaries.title: mqac.sys (MSMQ Access Control driver) - cross-branch rebuild; validation checks inlined from callees

changed_functions.name: CMMFAllocator::RestorePackets (0x140011814 unpatched / 0x1C00116D8 patched, free-space bitmap restore)
key_changes: Body differs only because the out-of-line helper CBitmap::FillBits (0x14000428C) was inlined into RestorePackets in the patched build; the capacity comparisons and the 0xA/0xB/0xC trace codes that appear inline in the patched build are the exact contents of CBitmap::FillBits, which the unpatched build calls out-of-line
CMMFAllocator::FindValidPacket (0x140010D68) and its CRC integrity check (CPacket::CheckPacket 0x1400159D0 -> CPacket::ComputeCRC) were likewise inlined in the patched build
No bounds check is added: CBitmap::FillBits already rejects start>capacity, count>capacity, and start+count>capacity before any write in the unpatched build
changed_functions.security_notes: The unpatched CMMFAllocator::RestorePackets updates the free-space bitmap through the out-of-line helper CBitmap::FillBits (0x14000428C). FillBits reads the bitmap capacity from the first dword of the CBitmap object and refuses the write when the start index, the count, or start+count exceed capacity (logging trace codes 0xA/0xB/0xC and returning without writing). The patched build inlined FillBits, so the same comparisons appear inline in RestorePackets. The check exists in both builds; the patch does not add it. Reachability: RestorePackets is a persistence-recovery path (ACDeviceControl 0x14000B100 -> ACRestorePackets 0x140009250 -> CPoolAllocator::RestorePackets 0x14001199C -> CMMFAllocator::RestorePackets); its indices derive from on-disk allocator geometry and bitmap contents, not a live message body.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
Unpatched CBitmap::FillBits (0x14000428C) capacity check that already exists:
  000000014000428C  test    r8d, r8d          ; count == 0 -> return
  00000001400042A9  mov     edi, [rcx]        ; edi = bitmap capacity
  00000001400042B4  cmp     r9d, edi          ; start > capacity ?
  00000001400042B7  ja      loc_140004421     ;   reject, no write
  00000001400042BD  cmp     r8d, edi          ; count > capacity ?
  00000001400042C0  ja      loc_140004421     ;   reject, no write
  00000001400042DC  cmp     eax, edi          ; start+count > capacity ?
  00000001400042DE  jbe     short loc_14000430C ; only here does the fill proceed
Patched RestorePackets (0x1C00116D8) contains the same comparisons and 0xA/0xB/0xC trace codes inline because FillBits was inlined.

changed_functions.name: CPacket::Create (0x14001620C unpatched / 0x1C001768C patched, packet create/dispatch)
key_changes: The transaction/queue-state validation returning 0xC00E0020 (MQ_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETER) and 0xC00E0006 exists in both builds; in the unpatched build it lives in the out-of-line helper ValidateProperties (0x14001A9F8), which CPacket::Create calls; the patched build inlined ValidateProperties
The 0xFFFFFFFF packet-buffer sentinel test exists in both builds; in the unpatched build it lives in CQEntry::Buffer (0x14000263C); the patched build inlined it
No new validation is added; the struct offsets checked (queue+0x58, sendparams+0xC0/0xE0/0x128/0x130/0x140/0x168, msgprops+0x38/0x58/0x11) are identical in both builds
changed_functions.security_notes: The unpatched CPacket::Create validates message/queue state by calling the out-of-line ValidateProperties (0x14001A9F8), which returns 0xC00E0020 and 0xC00E0006 for the same invalid state combinations that appear inline in the patched CPacket::Create. It obtains the packet buffer via CQEntry::Buffer (0x14000263C), whose 0xFFFFFFFF sentinel test and CMMFAllocator::GetAccessibleBuffer call the patched build inlines. Both the validation and the sentinel test are present in both builds; the patch adds neither.
changed_functions.assembly_diff: Unpatched ValidateProperties (0x14001A9F8) already returns 0xC00E0020 / 0xC00E0006 for the invalid state combinations. Unpatched CQEntry::Buffer (0x14000263C): if (*(entry+0x10)==0xFFFFFFFF) return null; else return CMMFAllocator::GetAccessibleBuffer(*(entry+0x18)). The patched CPacket::Create (0x1C001768C) contains both, inlined.

changed_functions.name: ACpBuildPacket (0x140013E24 unpatched / 0x1C001663C patched, packet body build/marshalling)
key_changes: The patched-side address 0x1C00181B8 cited earlier is a different function (CPacket::GetProperties), not the patched ACpBuildPacket; the correct patched ACpBuildPacket is 0x1C001663C

All body differences are inlined helpers with equivalent behavior: ProbeForRead expanded inline as the standard MmUserProbeAddress probe, section-size overflow checks pulled in from CalcSectionSize, SetProvInfoEx, and WOW64 pointer translators
No new bounds check, length validation, null guard, integer-overflow guard, or error-return path guarding a memory operation is present in the patched build that is absent from the unpatched build
changed_functions.security_notes: The 0xFFFFFFFF handle-sentinel check earlier attributed to ACpBuildPacket is not in ACpBuildPacket; it is in CQEntry::Buffer (0x14000263C), inlined into the patched CPacket::Create. ACpBuildPacket itself differs only by inlined helpers with equivalent behavior. The status 0xC000009A is STATUS_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES, not STATUS_INVALID_DEVICE_REQUEST.

changed_functions.name: CPacket::CompleteWriter (0x140015D94, IRP completion handler)
key_changes: Cross-branch restructure of IRP completion and lock release paths
changed_functions.security_notes: No memory-safety change; structural/branch difference between servicing branches.

changed_functions.name: CStorage::GetWriteRequest (0x140022B8C)
key_changes: Restructured control flow, cross-branch compiler differences

summary: No security-relevant change is demonstrable. The two files are different Windows servicing branches (mqac_unpatched.sys = 10.0.28000.2336, mqac_patched.sys = 10.0.19041.7052) compiled with different toolchains (28000 uses ExAllocatePool2, 19041 uses ExAllocatePoolWithTag) and different inlining, which accounts for the 61.97% similarity and the large number of changed function bodies. The bounds check, state-validation checks, and handle-sentinel check that an earlier body diff appeared to add are present in BOTH builds: in one build they live in out-of-line callees (CBitmap::FillBits at 0x14000428C for the bitmap capacity check; ValidateProperties at 0x14001A9F8 for the 0xC00E0020/0xC00E0006 state checks; CQEntry::Buffer at 0x14000263C for the 0xFFFFFFFF sentinel; CPacket::CheckPacket for the packet CRC), and in the other build the compiler inlined those callees into their callers. The instruction-level capacity check in CBitmap::FillBits (mov edi,[rcx]; cmp r9d,edi/ja; cmp r8d,edi/ja before any write) confirms the unpatched build already rejects out-of-range bitmap indices. No newly added bounds check, length/size validation, null/handle guard, integer-overflow guard, or error-return path guarding a memory operation exists in either build that is absent from the other. The earlier out-of-bounds-write finding, its exploit primitive, and the cross-function trigger chain are not supported by the binaries.
